title: Garlicky Nourishing Spanish Chicken Tortilla Espanola
description: A pillowy and flavorful Spanish-inspired chicken tortilla made with chicken drumsticks, garlic, and potatoes, perfect for a quick and satisfying meal.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced chicken drumsticks and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet and continue cooking for another 2 minutes.
Add the diced potatoes to the skillet and stir to combine with the chicken and onion mixture.
Season the mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the mixture to a large plate or tray and let cool slightly.
Using a mortar and pestle or a fork, pound the cooked chicken mixture into small pieces.
Beat the egg in a large bowl and stir in the pounded chicken mixture, chopped parsley, and a pinch of salt.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil in the same skillet over medium heat.
Once the oil is hot, pour the egg mixture into the skillet and cook until the edges start to set, about 2 minutes.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the tortilla towards the center, allowing the uncooked egg to flow to the edges.
Continue cooking the tortilla for another 2-3 minutes, or until it is almost set and the edges are golden brown.
Slide the tortilla onto a serving plate and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Cook the tortilla for an additional 2-3 minutes on the other side, or until it is cooked through and fluffy in the center.
